My name is Orin Klaff and I'm here today because on one hand I've lost hundreds of deals made terrible mistakes but I've also revisited those mistakes and fixed a lot of the problems we all make when we pitch a deal trying to influence others. So think about it this way you walk into a meeting and immediately each person assesses two things right one who has the most power and two who is the most needy and over many experiences buyers your buyers have learned that they have the power most of the time within seconds you figure out the buyer has the money therefore he has the power and unfortunately you are the needy party. All of this pushes you into the low status position a position you will spend the rest of the meeting trying to dig out from but hey that's just the law of supply and demand right they have the money so they have the power it has to be this way buyers have money and money is power. When I ask does it really have to work this way because if you look at the buyer sell a relationship to the lens of Louis Vuitton, Goldman Sachs and the motorcycle manufacturer Ducati life is really quite different their buyers their buyers get in line they're told what they can buy and at what price and when and these buyers do so without complaint because they they appreciate the opportunity it's it's not just premium brands I mentioned that have this kind of buyer control like for example I know a paper salesman he sells printer paper so does Staples, Rite Aid, Walmart but my guy has inverted the power flow between himself and the customer and he is always busy. I showed him how to do this because he's a friend but it's the same thing I do for CEOs of large companies for venture capital firms and for large ad agencies right so in this segment what we're going to do in a minute I'm going to show you specifically the process of rotating the cycle of power 180 degrees and this is called pricing right this is why I call it pricing. Imagine I'm a prospective buyer of your stuff and I've asked you to come see me for a meeting so you drive 50 miles to my Los Angeles office you park walk into my really non-descript building and you take the elevator to number 302 you come through the doorway into a room overlooking the Grand Hollywood Hills you sit at a long conference table and I say to you go and now you start selling right but what are you really doing you are trying to win a prize the prize is my money my order my acceptance in this situation I'm the prize and unfortunately you're the supplicant right what's a supplicant you know a supplicant is a person who says oh I'm gonna do whatever it takes to earn your business a supplicant says let me prove to you we're the right supplier for you a supplicant allows price to be the main topic of conversation whether it's on the internet or in person right a supplicant submits a proposal for the buyer to review and consider at his own leisure look I have done these things we all have they are common because it's it's natural to act this way it's the it's the natural order of things to appease those with money and power so they will buy our products and like us and invest in our deal and tell others about our ideas so even though this truly is the natural order why should you submit to what's natural normal or really even pre-coded in your own DNA right we're hunter-gatherers it's not natural for us to build a museum like the Guggenheim it's not normal for us to travel into space it's not in our DNA to have a international monetary fund to help weak countries but we do these things because we can rise above our primal natural selves to do great things and to contain the beast within so I submit for your acceptance the skill of pricing a way for you to rise above your primal human nature right pricing is is a subtle reframing technique reframing is a way of changing the other person's perspective right so they can see the world through your lens your perspective your point of view pricing is a way to deal with powerful buyers who can push you again into the low status position and then give you just a small and limited amount of their attention all of this makes your job of selling just so difficult time-consuming and worst of all in my mind not fun so pricing is the act of shifting or reframing the selling environment so the buyer sees you as the prize that he must win being a successful marketer presenter or salesperson is a task that I know it already places extraordinary demands on your ability to manage time and money and people's emotions but if you remain in the shadow of the buyer and you never establish yourself as the prize then that others must win then you will always be soft weak and you will only get the business when others are feeling generous and they give it to you so look it's a harsh and unforgiving world out there I'm an investment banker okay it's harsh and unforgiving world and it will only get worse if you cannot establish yourself as the prize so seeing a problem and understanding how to solve it are two different things so hopefully now you see the pricing problem but let's solve it let's move into understanding how to prize and there are three main principles to this and seven basic steps so principle number one first understand the the programming encoding the genetic instruction set of every human mind number one we chase that which moves away from us we want that which we cannot have and we only place value on things that are difficult to obtain that's the way the mind works here's the second principle as you can see I'm not a motivational speaker I don't care about your purity of heart your positive mental attitude or your empowering belief systems to me that's just mumbo jumbo I only care about the way of the sword I care that you get in your mind and in your gut this one thing money is a commodity this isn't philosophy it's a hard economic proof point it may be hard to get meetings where buyers give you their money but ultimately that's all they have money and that that is available anywhere I mean need some money right get a loan from one of 8,000 banks in the country find find an investor spend less on other stuff you buy like contrast this with other commodities gas rice pork those are hundreds of times more difficult to source and find and trade money is the is the ultimate commodity it's available anywhere you should never compromise your price your time your values and certainly not your fun to get this plentiful commodity the moment I'm going to well this this idea to your behavior and and when I do that it's when you and I will win because by winning I just mean one thing our pitch to close ratio gets better winning means your pitch to close ratio goes up right that's what winning is that's the way of the sword right so how do you communicate to the buyer that their money is a commodity that you are like Louis Vuitton Bugatti or last year's Heisman trophy winner right that you are the most valuable asset in the relationship that the buyer must propose to you why he is a good customer and he must explain why you should agree to work with him no matter what the price he was willing to pay how is it done once you believe that money is a commodity the right words will just it will tumble from your lips I only work with good customers that are fun and they let me profit and what kind of customer are you that's what you'll be saying right so principle number three you have to eradicate your neediness so you can tell when you're losing an audience right because they're they're growing discomfort is easy to read they glance at their watch they move their mouse elsewhere on the screen if you sell on the internet they close the folder they've been looking through and there are there are lots of signs and when the buyer starts to move away from you you have a natural reaction of fear the deal is slipping away in this moment and more than any other time in a presentation we want to need the buyer to accept us validate us right and this is the moment when we have trapped ourselves we the more we want the buyer to validate us the more neediness we broadcast the less likely they are to give us what we want right there's a name for this engineers call this the negative feedback loop negative feedback loop can destroy bridges fall over buildings and it can take down an energy grid it can easily mess up your deal so if acting needy right if it has been my greatest problem over the years which it has right then eradicating neediness has also become my greatest accomplishment I say this because like you I'm a geek maybe like you you know I'm not a natural savant or a Jedi I wasn't born knowing this stuff I need steps to follow and the fewer the better here's what I discovered to eradicate neediness from your game one want nothing want nothing care about delivering great value no matter what feedback you're getting from your audience great value and be willing to walk away no matter how much you need the deal even if you will lose money look bad or starve because the moment you need a deal you begin to lose it give you a quick example several years ago when I was just learning this stuff I was the junior partner on a twenty million dollar deal and we needed to close just one final investor I'll never forget it Andrew we just need to close Andrew and the deal would be done and it was my job to get that investor closed but I couldn't do it so I called the senior partner Russell Russ I can't get this guy to sign the release docs you know the legal stuff please jump on the phone and get this deal done we can close today if he signs up we really need this I need it we need this guy so can we get on the phone and talk to him and Russ says yes I'll get on the phone with you and these two guys get on the phone and in less than a minute they're fighting oh man so so then Russ says to the investor Andrew Andy here's what you need to do okay take this contract and I was so happy because I needed this deal and I needed to make myself look good and here was Russ stopping fighting with the guy and is about to close it and make everything work perfectly and Russ continued Andy take this contract and shove it up your ass whoa what just happened I called Russell right after that what did you do that for right because because he says we don't need anyone to close our deal it's still gonna close today because you're gonna get out your checkbook and I'm gonna get on my checkbook and you and I are gonna close this deal we're gonna invest in the final piece and from now on Orin we are never gonna go into a call needy all right so as you may be seeing the relationship between two people trying to buy and sell something from each other has a distinctive signature a pattern of behavior and this is how it can be read and decoded as I and probably others like me who study this stuff have done it's like the tap tap tap tap tap of a Morse code message a business deal has an identifiable pattern and part of the pattern is that one person needs the deal more than the other so therefore one is the supplicant and one is the prize it has to be this way it's a game a cycle it's a primal encoded unchangeable rule set but as in most games who is the winner is not predecided it's not manifest underdogs can and do win you can change the social dynamic and become the prize so to achieve the price status let's talk about what to do exactly step by step step one time constraint time constraint this deal is available for a certain period of time and then it's gone think of Amtrak the train leaves at 3.03 and at 3.04 it's not there the train is leaving the station number two you have to establish your celebrity status or your star power right you do this by providing insight let me tell you how this business is changing Mr. Customer Mr. Buyer Mr. Investor and how you can understand these changes and benefit from them step three this will this will raise your status right so you'll lock in your high status by interpreting the change right these changes mean for you that you there's a market window that you can invest now or buy now and get a better deal than you could otherwise this is generic information you're not selling right you're raising your status by adding value and step four is you're going to hold the buyers attention by making your messaging novel fast and visual if you make a novel they'll listen for longer than they would normally and if you do it fast then they'll appreciate getting clear crisp information on a good timeline and if you make it visual then it'll be easy for them to understand for them to understand step five establish scarcity for yourself not the product or the deal for yourself I only have so much time and therefore I can only take on so many customers step six is when you've done all this other stuff right then you're going to apply counter pressure you'll be the prize that's very simple tell me why I should choose you from among all the customers I can choose to work with all right and step seven you're going to make the buyer perform a legitimate task so you you know you make them earn their way into your deal BMW makes people do that on some of the cars you have to sign a form before you buy it that you promise to take care of the paint job all right and step eight is easy make it clear in firm but not unfriendly ways that you are willing to just walk away okay I don't need this deal and I'm willing to walk away from it if it gets too difficult once you have done this you're the prize and you'll proceed normally just the mechanics of the sale there's no pressure after that no sales tactics and there's no trick knowledge none of that stuff will be needed right the more you can do this the easier it is to prize and there's more that can be done but these are the basics to pricing these steps are how you will protect your status protect your value and and when you do it you'll be well respected right others will want to work with you I wouldn't they and this will be more fun for you and all the people you work with I know that's the way it is for me I mean will it work every time no of course not nothing can and nothing even should but it will increase your pitch to close ratio business people that know how to prize look they're not just doing a little better than the competition they're leaving them in the dust so if for you certain of these practices seem contrary to what you know to the doctrine you're familiar with then maybe it's the doctrine that needs to be refreshed and reexamined all right let's do that together
